message:
fix(c-api): wrap sqlite3_step execution in catch_unwind to prevent panic propagation across FFI boundary

Refactor the sqlite3_step C API function to catch Rust panics before they
cross the FFI boundary (which is undefined behavior). The query/execute
dispatch is now wrapped in std::panic::catch_unwind with AssertUnwindSafe,
and panic results are translated into SQLITE_ERROR with a descriptive
"Rust panic during statement execution" error message.

This also simplifies the control flow by unifying the Query and Execute
match arms: Query maps results to Some(rows) while Execute maps to None,
allowing a single match expression to handle success/error for both paths
instead of duplicating error handling logic in each arm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
